summary: The CI build failed during compilation of pandas' SIMD/NEON C++ sources on macOS arm64. The clang++ compiler emitted errors about invalid explicit template instantiations in the SIMD moments headers, causing ninja to abort and pip metadata generation to fail.
root_cause: clang++ reports 'explicit instantiation of operator() does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function template, or static data member template' in pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp at line 255 and in pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp at line 14, producing 2 compiler errors that abort the NEON and base SIMD library builds.

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp:255:50: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, m…
evidence.reason: This is the primary compiler error — clang++ rejects an explicit instantiation of operator() in the SIMD header, indicating the template specialization syntax is invalid for this compiler version.

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp:14:43: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member funct…
evidence.reason: A second distinct compiler error in the NEON instantiation file at line 14, confirming the explicit instantiation pattern used is rejected by the arm64/macOS clang++ toolchain.
ninja reports the NEON object file compilation failed with exit code 1, directly caused by the above compiler errors.
The base moments object file also failed to compile for the same reason, showing the issue affects both NEON and base SIMD build targets.
Clang confirms exactly 2 errors were generated for the NEON translation unit, matching the two error lines above.
ninja aborted the entire build due to the compiler errors, propagating the failure up to pip.
Identifies the toolchain as arm64 Apple clang++, relevant because explicit instantiation rules for operator() may differ from other platforms/compilers.
The build was configured with --werror, meaning any warning would also be fatal; however the log shows these are outright errors, not warnings promoted to errors.
pip's top-level failure is a downstream consequence of the ninja/compiler failure, confirming the compile error is the root cause.

suggested_fix: Fix the explicit instantiation of 'operator()' in 'pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp' (line 255) and 'pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp' (line 14) to use a valid explicit instantiation form accepted by clang++ on arm64-apple-darwin — for example, replace the explicit instantiation of the call operator with an explicit instantiation of the enclosing class template instead.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
